pattern: Every length-k window having xor zero forces the array to be periodic with period k and one period to xor to zero, reducing the task to a column-wise knapsack over achievable xor values.
hints: Two adjacent windows of size k differ by one element entering and one leaving, so all windows share the same xor only if positions k apart are equal.
Group the indices by their remainder modulo k into k columns; within a column every value should become the same, and the k chosen column values must xor to zero.

Run a DP over columns where state is the running xor of chosen column values and the cost is how many entries you must change; combine a per-column frequency table with the best result so far to fill in the cheapest assignment.

edge_inputs.description: k equals one, every element must become zero except keep the most frequent
single element with k one already xors to zero only if zero
single nonzero element must change to zero
already periodic and xor zero needs no change
k equals length, only need the whole xor to be zero

stress.description: small array over the full value domain with a moderate period
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 60))
    nums = [rng.randrange(0, 1024) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    return [nums, k]
